defects.finding: Windows orchestration imported and called the Wildberries dispatcher directly.
defects.closure: Dispatch now uses a marketplace-neutral request and registry.

defects.finding: No enforceable adapter identity, registration or dispatch contract existed.
defects.closure: Added protocol, request envelope, canonical IDs, frozen registry and result validation.

defects.finding: Concrete adapter output had no common invariant preventing marketplace writes.
defects.closure: Registry rejects write-enabled results and always emits marketplace_write_enabled=false.

defects.finding: Missing marketplace identity was not enforced at the source bridge boundary.
defects.closure: Missing or invalid marketplace IDs fail closed with explicit reason codes.

defects.finding: Ozon had no independent adapter boundary.
defects.closure: Registered an independent Ozon adapter that blocks semantic processing until approved source profiles exist.
defects.limitation: Ozon financial parsing remains intentionally unavailable.

defects.finding: No regression gate prevented concrete marketplace dependencies from returning to core layers.
defects.closure: Added architecture source-boundary tests for finance, insights, recommendations, pilot and adapter layers.

defects.finding: Four legacy recommendation tests patched the removed Wildberries module seam and failed before exercising the neutral registry boundary.
defects.closure: Tests now inject a MarketplaceAdapterRegistry mock and provide explicit marketplace identity; production code remains marketplace-neutral.
